reasoning: The Lean formalization matches the informal statement. The hypotheses correctly encode "H is a maximal normal abelian subgroup": H is normal (h_normal), H is abelian (h_comm, using Mathlib's IsMulCommutative which expresses commutativity of multiplication), and H is maximal among normal abelian subgroups (h_maximal_normal_abelian: any normal abelian K with H ≤ K equals H). The conclusion encodes "H is a maximal abelian subgroup": any abelian subgroup K with H ≤ K equals H (with H itself already known abelian via h_comm). The ambient hypotheses (p prime, G finite group, G is a p-group via IsPGroup) faithfully encode the setup. The encoding of "maximal X" as "any X-subgroup containing H equals H" is the standard and faithful way to express maximality.
